reasons: MV-job-discovery-002 (HIGH) CONFIRMED FIXED: page.tsx old single `bulkApply(explicitIds?)` function that POSTed on click is fully removed and replaced by requestBulkApply(ids, trigger) (page.tsx:479-485 in eccfdb7 blob) which only stages bulkGateIds/opens bulkGateOpen — no network call — and confirmBulkApply() (page.tsx:492-512) which performs the `for (const id of ids) apiRequest(POST /jobs/{id}/apply)` loop, gated behind the confirm button's onClick only (page.tsx:1413-1414). BOTH call sites route through the gate: list 'Apply (N)' button (page.tsx:844-849, onClick calls requestBulkApply) and SavedView's 'Apply to all' (page.tsx:1467-1471, onApplyAll prop now typed (ids, trigger) and wired at page.tsx:813 to requestBulkApply). grep of the eccfdb7 blob for `bulkApply` shows zero remaining references to a bare/silent apply function — only requestBulkApply/confirmBulkApply exist. Dialog (page.tsx:1356-1429) discloses 'current, untailored resume' and 'This action cannot be undone' (page.tsx:1385-1389), lists every staged job title+company (page.tsx:1380-1391), and requires explicit Cancel/Confirm; ESC and backdrop-click also route to closeBulkGate (no accidental apply). No silent mass-apply POST loop remains outside the gated confirm handler.

MV-job-discovery-004 (MEDIUM) CONFIRMED FIXED, not dead: Role filter is a real title substring match against live job data (page.tsx:298 `if (roleQuery && !(j.title ?? '').toLowerCase().includes(roleQuery.toLowerCase())) return false;`). Salary filter compares a real band threshold against `salaryMax ?? salaryMin` (page.tsx:299-304). Verified these are NOT mock/dead fields: apps/api/app/repositories/job.py:11-12 selects `"salaryMin", "salaryMax", "currency"` directly from the Job table and apps/api/app/routers/jobs.py:34-58 `list_jobs` returns raw dicts with no filtering, and the frontend's job-list fetch at page.tsx:259 uses raw `apiRequest<Job[]>` (bypassing the zod-validated `fetchJobs()` wrapper in lib/api/jobs.ts that would otherwise strip unknown keys) — so salaryMin/salaryMax survive end-to-end from DB to filter. Both filters reset via `clearAll()` (page.tsx: roleQuery/salaryMinFilter reset alongside existing filters), tested and passing.

MV-job-discovery-005 (LOW) CONFIRMED FIXED: honest note only renders when `rejected.length > 0` (page.tsx ~1207), states 'N of M suggestions applied ... rejected by the fabrication guard because they couldn't be verified against your real experience, so nothing unsupported was added to your resume' — computed from real `tailorResults[id].changes`/`.rejected.length`, not a static string. Does not appear when nothing was rejected (no dead placeholder always shown).

Tests are non-tautological: independently re-ran the new 7-test file against the pre-fix parent commit (ec20b91) in a separate worktree and confirmed all 7 FAIL there (bulk gate/testids don't exist, role/salary filters have no effect, tailor-rejected-note absent) while the 1 pre-existing unrelated test still passes — this is fresh evidence from this review run, not trust in the fixer's commit message claim.

Full apps/web vitest suite (57 files / 412 tests), `tsc --noEmit`, and `next build` all green at eccfdb7, independently re-run in a fresh git worktree with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (not reusing the fixer's worktree/build artifacts).

No prohibited patterns found: grep across the full diff for TODO/FIXME/@ts-ignore/eslint-disable/' any'/'as any'/Math.random/--no-verify/console.log returned zero matches; no skipped tests (.skip/xit/xdescribe/it.todo); no tautological assertions (expect(true), etc). The one `as Job & {salaryMin?...}` type-intersection cast at page.tsx:302 is a narrow, pre-existing codebase pattern (identical cast already used at page.tsx:116 for salary display, untouched by this diff) — not a new broad `any` cast, so not flagged as prohibited.

No regression or security risk identified: the bulk-apply gate reduces risk (adds a confirmation step where none existed) rather than introducing one; the per-id sequential POST loop inside confirmBulkApply is unchanged behavior from the pre-fix bulkApply (same apiRequest POST semantics), just moved behind explicit confirmation — not a new attack surface.
